This earthquake has shifted the floorboards, making it impossible to maintain my concealed position.
The floodwaters are rising quickly, and my target is using the chaos to escape.
I must find a stable vantage point before the aftershocks bring this entire building down.
Mudslides have blocked the primary extraction route, leaving me stranded with a dead mark.
The howling wind from the storm is masking my footsteps, giving me a perfect advantage.
The flashing light in the sky is a distraction that might alert my target to my presence.
I must find a shadow to blend into before the sudden brightness reveals my position.
This celestial anomaly is irrelevant unless it provides a cover for me to move closer.
My focus remains on the mark, regardless of the strange colors bleeding across the horizon.
If the target looks up at the stars, that is the perfect moment to strike.
This fence line is irrelevant because your neck is exposed from this specific angle.
I do not care where the property ends as long as the target remains visible.
Your arguments about acreage will cease the moment my blade finds its mark.
I have already mapped every blind spot in this garden for the final strike.
Keep talking about the boundary while I calculate the windage for the long shot.
I will keep my blade sheathed until I determine if the target has stepped through this shimmering rift.
There are no shadows to cling to here, leaving me exposed to any sentries waiting on the other side.
If this gateway leads to the mark's sanctuary, I must enter silently and leave no trace of my presence.
My coin is paid, and whether this portal leads to another realm or a void, the contract must be fulfilled.
I shall test the perimeter of the glow with a throwing knife to see if it deflects or consumes.
I must keep my boots from snapping dry twigs to avoid alerting the target.
The canopy is thick enough to mask my silhouette as I track their scent.
I will wait for the wind to howl before I release the crossbow bolt.
These thorns are tearing at my cloak, but the cover is worth the blood.
I can see the flickering campfire through the brush, marking the perfect strike point.
My breathing is a shallow rhythm, barely disturbing the dust of this rafters' perch.
Every muscle in my legs screams from the stillness, but the target has not yet emerged.
I have counted ten thousand heartbeats since I last shifted my grip on the blade.
The silence is a heavy cloak that hides the sound of my pulse from the guards below.
One single misplaced exhale will betray my position and ruin months of careful planning.
You have compromised my position, but you are far too slow to stop the blade.
My contract remains active, and your sudden aggression only confirms that you are the target.
I have already mapped every exit in this room while you were busy threatening me.
A direct confrontation is inefficient, yet I will ensure your heart stops before you blink.
Your voice is loud, but your guard is open, leaving you completely exposed to my strike.
This dampened blade is the only tool that never fails to slide through a ribcage.
My customized garrote wire is thin enough to be invisible but strong enough to crush windpipes.
I keep this poison vial tucked away because some targets are too guarded for a knife.
These soft-soled boots allow me to cross a marble floor without making a single sound.
This long-range rifle is my most valued asset when the target is behind reinforced glass.
My remaining poison vials are nearly empty, and I must choose the target most worthy of the final dose.
These silver coins are dwindling fast, leaving me barely enough to bribe the guard at the eastern gate.
I have only three bolts left in my quiver to clear this corridor before the alarm is raised.
My breath is shallow and my stamina is fading, yet I must climb this tower before dawn breaks.
The darkness is my only cover, and I cannot waste my few remaining smoke bombs on a minor distraction.
